commit_message: I'm checking in this state before tearing it all down

user_prompt: the highlighted javascript function should detect if the page is served as an offline test, like maybe it's served via "file:\\\" or something like that
agent_reply: Updated [html/script.js](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:173). `is_offline_test()` now returns `true` when the page is loaded from:
- `file:`
- `localhost`
and `false` otherwise. Also normalized the file back to CRLF.

user_prompt: if is_offline_test() is false then hide all of the templates
implement the highlighted logic in javascript
agent_reply: Implemented in [html/script.js](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:167).
`body_onload()` now calls `hide_all_test_divs()` only when `is_offline_test()` is false, and `hide_all_test_divs()` hides elements whose ids end in `_test`, like `files_test`.
Kept CRLF line endings intact.

user_prompt: if under an offline test `#login_panel` is not hidden, otherwise, the `body_onload()` function hides `#login_panel`. The completetion of `/get_info` finds out the security level, and then `#login_panel` is shown or hidden accordingly
agent_reply: Implemented in [html/script.js](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:167).
Behavior now matches what you described:
- Offline test: `#login_panel` stays visible on load.
- Normal served page: `body_onload()` immediately hides `#login_panel`.
- When `/get_info` completes and `current_security_level` is known:
- security level `0`: login panel stays hidden.
- any other level: login panel is shown.
- Successful login now hides the panel instead of removing it from the DOM.
Also kept the offline test fixtures visible during offline tests and normalized `html/script.js` to CRLF. I tried `node --check`, but Node is not installed in this environment.
Implemented in [html/script.js](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:167).
Behavior now matches what you described:
- Offline test: `#login_panel` stays visible on load.
- Normal served page: `body_onload()` immediately hides `#login_panel`.
- When `/get_info` completes and `current_security_level` is known:
- security level `0`: login panel stays hidden.
- any other level: login panel is shown.
- Successful login now hides the panel instead of removing it from the DOM.
Also kept the offline test fixtures visible during offline tests and normalized `html/script.js` to CRLF. I tried `node --check`, but Node is not installed in this environment.

user_prompt: everything highlighted here, nothing more, nothing less, is broken out into another function that accepts an object, later I could theoretically call it from the developer tools with a test object
agent_reply: Done. Extracted the highlighted block into [html/script.js](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:452):
```js
function handle_info(info)
`fetch_info()` now parses the response and calls `handle_info(info)`, so you can later call `handle_info({...})` from dev tools with a test object. CRLF preserved.

user_prompt: you've already made `set_password_reset_message`, make `function set_login_error(message)` work in a similar fashion
agent_reply: Done. `set_login_error(message, notice)` now behaves like the reset message helpers:
- writes to `#login_error`
- toggles `.notice`
- hides itself when the message is empty
- no more `window.alert()` for login/config errors

I also added the `#login_error` element to [html/index.html](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/index.html:32), reused the existing message styling in [html/styles.css](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/styles.css:251), and clear the message when the login panel is hidden. CRLF preserved.

user_prompt: `handle_info` should automatically trigger a ajax request to `/list_files.json`, which is served by `WebFileHandlers::sendMicroSdFileList`, the result should unhide and populate `#files_test` after using `make_rows_from_template`
agent_reply: Done. `handle_info(info)` now calls `fetch_file_list()` after finishing the existing info/security setup.
Added:
- `fetch_file_list()` AJAX GET to `/list_files.json`
- `render_file_list(files)` to populate `#files_test`
- `make_rows_from_template("files_table_body", ...)` usage for file rows
- `files_table_body` `<tbody>` in [html/index.html](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/index.html:42)
- cached file-row template so empty file lists don’t destroy the reusable template
- hides `#files_please_wait` and unhides `#files_test` when the list renders
Touched files are CRLF-normalized.

user_prompt: in `handle_info` somewhere, for security level 1 and over, if `default_soft_ap` is false, the `#login_panel` panel becomes hidden, `function apply_login_panel_visibility()` needs to beware of this
also if `default_soft_ap` is false, all the `class="please-login-first"` items say "reconfiguration not allowed", we'll tweak this message later
also `#btn_save` needs to be shown and hidden appropriately as per the above rules
agent_reply: Implemented in [html/script.js](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:22).
`handle_info(info)` now stores `default_soft_ap` state and applies one shared visibility path:
- `default_soft_ap === false` hides `#login_panel`
- `.please-login-first` elements change to `reconfiguration not allowed`
- `#btn_save` is hidden when reconfiguration is not allowed
- `#btn_save` is shown only when reconfiguration is allowed and config is unlocked:
- security level `0`, or
- successful login for security level `1+`
I also made `hide_logged_out_markers()` respect the reconfiguration-disabled state so it won’t hide those “reconfiguration not allowed” messages later. CRLF preserved.
Implemented in [html/script.js](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:22).
`handle_info(info)` now stores `default_soft_ap` state and applies one shared visibility path:
- `default_soft_ap === false` hides `#login_panel`
- `.please-login-first` elements change to `reconfiguration not allowed`
- `#btn_save` is hidden when reconfiguration is not allowed
- `#btn_save` is shown only when reconfiguration is allowed and config is unlocked:
- security level `0`, or
- successful login for security level `1+`
I also made `hide_logged_out_markers()` respect the reconfiguration-disabled state so it won’t hide those “reconfiguration not allowed” messages later. CRLF preserved.

user_prompt: `passreset_onsubmit()` please enforce minimum 6 character password with at least one letter (don't care about case) and one number, and no spaces
agent_reply: Done. `passreset_onsubmit()` now rejects reset passwords that:
- are shorter than 6 characters
- contain spaces or any whitespace
- do not contain at least one letter
- do not contain at least one number

It reports the reason through `set_password_reset_message(...)` before checking confirmation or sending the request. CRLF preserved. Node is still not installed here, so I couldn’t run a JS syntax check.

- do not contain at least one letter
- do not contain at least one number

It reports the reason through `set_password_reset_message(...)` before checking confirmation or sending the request. CRLF preserved. Node is still not installed here, so I couldn’t run a JS syntax check.

user_prompt:
the javascript causes the `` element of the page be completely empty except `

Password Reset Sucessful

please allow the device to reboot`
the handler `void reset_password(AsyncWebServerRequest* request)` should show a `ModalDialog` view with a confirmation. Dismissing the dialog causes the whole microcontroller to reboot
agent_reply: Implemented.
In [html/script.js](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:879), successful `/reset_password` now replaces the entire page body with only:
```html
Password Reset Sucessful
please allow the device to reboot

In [lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:92), successful `reset_password()` now shows a `ModalDialog` confirmation using the thumbs-up sprite. Dismissing that dialog calls `esp_restart()`.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1` compiled the changed C++ and reached link.
- Link failed with existing size pressure: `.iram0.text` overflowed by `2152 bytes`.
- `pio run` default env still fails earlier because `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` is undefined for `thefly`.
- CRLF preserved.
PlatformIO also regenerated [inc/web_assets.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h) when packaging the updated HTML/JS.

user_prompt: when memory reset is successful, same as the last task about password reset but for the memory reset function

the javascript causes the `` element of the page be completely empty except `

Memory Reset Sucessful

please allow the device to reboot`
the handler `void reset_memory(AsyncWebServerRequest* request)` should show a `ModalDialog` view with a confirmation. Dismissing the dialog causes the whole microcontroller to reboot
agent_reply: Implemented the memory reset flow to match the password reset behavior.
Changes made:
- `reset_memory()` now shows a `ModalDialog` confirmation after successful memory reset.
- Dismissing that dialog calls `esp_restart()` so the microcontroller reboots.
- The web UI now replaces the page body with:

`

Memory Reset Sucessful

please allow the device to reboot`
- Shared reset dialog helper logic is used for both password reset and memory reset.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed successfully.
- `inc/web_assets.h` was regenerated by the build.
- `thefly_sec1` still hits an existing IRAM overflow at link time, unrelated to this change.

commit_message: good progress on front end, organized the js files a bit, password and memory reset implemented
